title: brain sync reset [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: The operator-authorized OQ#5 escape hatch: re-converge the broker-owned canonical ref (git.canonical_ref, refs/atlas/main) to the current upstream tree over the note globs after a divergence REJECT halt (or a deterministic exit-3 block whose offending artifact is now absent upstream). TREE-reconcile, not history-replay: it diffs canonicalTree vs upstreamTree, builds ONE reconciled commit as a CHILD of the current canonical head (always a fast-forward), scans every absorbed byte, archives notes present only in the old canonical tree, and re-baselines the cursor to the upstream head — accepting (and auditing) the history gap. Two exclusive modes: --export-challenge does scanned, READ-ONLY planning and emits the broker AuthorizationChallenge (exit 0, zero mutation); --authorization <path> applies a signed challenge. A mutating invocation with neither is action-required (exit 6). --yes never authorizes. Privileged (broker-signature); the challenge binds the reconciliation via intendedEffect.changePlanDigest (a hash of canonicalBase + upstreamHead + note globs + the reconciled writes/deletes/quarantined set) + canonicalBaseCommit, so any drift in canonical head, upstream head, note globs, or the reconciliation plan changes the bound digest and the broker refuses before any mutation. (No targetCommit is bound: the reconciled child commit's sha is only known after the worktree commit, so the deterministic plan digest is the binding — functionally stronger than a single sha.)

mode.description: 'export-challenge' = the read-only plan+challenge emission (no mutation); 'applied' = a signed authorization was verified and the reconcile committed.

reBaselinedTo.description: The upstream head OID the cursor was re-baselined to (mode 'applied'); the head the reset WOULD re-baseline to under 'export-challenge'. null only if the upstream ref does not resolve (which fails before this envelope).

upstreamRef.description: The row's upstream_ref (the single upstream authority).

archived.description: Notes present only in the OLD canonical tree (absent from the reconciled upstream tree) → ProposeArchive (non-destructive, chunks dropped on the enqueued reconcile drain). May be empty.

captured.description: Notes present in the upstream tree that the reconcile absorbed (add/modify), content-addressed, scan-before-persist. May be empty (history-only reset).

quarantined.description: Upstream-tree paths whose bytes failed the scan — quarantined, not absorbed. The 'canonical tree equals upstream' guarantee holds only over NON-quarantined paths; a non-empty quarantined set makes reset exit 6 (partial reconcile). quarantineId is "" under --export-challenge.

pendingQuarantine.description: The reconciled durable pending-quarantine set after the reset (self-reports the pending state for the partial-reconcile/exit-6 case).

reconcileJobId.description: The enqueued index:reconcile job id (idempotencyKey = the reconciled canonical commit OID); null on a history-only/empty reset (nothing to re-index) and under --export-challenge.

cycleSeq.description: sync_cursors.cycle_seq after the reset (post-increment in mode 'applied'); the current unchanged value under --export-challenge.

historyGapAccepted.description: true when the reset re-baselined the cursor across a non-ancestral/unreachable divergence (an accepted history gap, audited as 'accepted-history-gap'); false when it recovered a deterministic exit-3 block with ancestry intact ('deterministic-scan-halt-recovery').

x-atlas-contract.summary: Operator-authorized tree-reconcile of the canonical ref to the upstream tree after a divergence/exit-3 halt: scan-before-persist, delete→archive, one FF canonical advance to a reconciled child commit, cursor re-baseline + one index:reconcile enqueue, accepted history gap audited. Two exclusive modes: --export-challenge (read-only) / --authorization.

flags.description: Scanned READ-ONLY planning: emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge bound to canonicalBaseCommit + intendedEffect.changePlanDigest (the deterministic reconciliation-plan hash), mutate nothing (no ref/cursor/projection/audit/quarantine write). Sign it out of band, then apply with --authorization. Exit 0.

Apply a signed AuthorizationChallenge: the broker re-verifies (canonicalBaseCommit CAS + the re-derived changePlanDigest matches the signed one + signer/nonce) immediately before the single canonical advance. A drift (canonical moved ⇒ authz.canonical_moved; upstream tree / note globs / reconciliation plan changed ⇒ different changePlanDigest ⇒ authz.target_mismatch; a concurrent cursor advance ⇒ the finalize cycle_seq CAS) is refused before any mutation. --yes never substitutes for this.

locks.note: during the single authorized broker advance

sideEffects: one run (run.planned then run.integrated broker-signed) when the reconcile has a non-empty ChangePlan and a valid authorization
one authorized broker CAS fast-forward of the configured canonical ref to the reconciled child commit
sync_cursors re-baseline (last_absorbed_oid = upstream head, cycle_seq++, reconciled pending_quarantine) under a cycle_seq CAS, in the finalize transaction
one index:reconcile enqueue (idempotencyKey = reconciled canonical OID) over the union of changed notes, atomic with the cursor finalize
quarantine records (AEAD, ciphertext-only) for upstream-tree paths that fail the scan
prohibitedEffects: any mutation under --export-challenge (read-only plan + challenge only)
any mutation without a verified authorization (--yes never authorizes)
writes refs/heads/main (or any upstream ref — only the configured canonical ref advances, via the broker)
a non-fast-forward canonical rewrite (the reconciled commit is a CHILD of the current canonical head)
ProposeDelete/erase for a note absent upstream (archive only)

errorCodes.when: both --export-challenge and --authorization, or an unknown flag
a non-terminal per-path error aborts the reconcile; the canonical ref and cursor are unchanged
a mutating invocation with neither --export-challenge nor --authorization
the canonical ref is not an adopted broker-owned ref, or equals the row's upstream_ref
the vault repo/upstream ref is unresolvable or the sync_cursors row is absent/malformed
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks ledger-writing runs
a non-attributable dirty verdict, or a deterministic exit-3 artifact still present in the upstream tree (reset is not a scan override); cursor unadvanced
broker drift: the canonical tip moved from the challenge's canonicalBaseCommit (a concurrent capture) — no mutation
broker drift: the re-derived intendedEffect.changePlanDigest differs from the signed challenge (upstream tree, note globs, or reconciliation plan changed since signing) — no mutation
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: Ed25519 signature verification failed
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
broker: the challenge/response fails the contracts schema (universal broker validation failure)
broker: the response's payloadCanonicalization id is unknown (universal broker validation failure)
broker: signerId is not in the signer registry
broker: the signer is revoked
broker: the signer is not permitted for the sync reset op
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
≥1 attributable upstream path was quarantined (partial reconcile); the cursor still re-baselines over non-quarantined paths
